ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.instruction: Write a prompt asking AI to help you brainstorm ideas for a creative project. Be specific about your medium, audience, and what kind of help you need.
ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.placeholder: e.g., I'm a graphic designer working on...
ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.hint: I'm a children's book illustrator brainstorming concepts for a story about a shy octopus. Give me 8 visual scene ideas that mix watercolor textures with humor. Target age: 4-7.
ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.goodExample: I'm a ceramicist designing a new mug collection inspired by brutalist architecture. Brainstorm 6 handle designs that reference concrete forms but are comfortable to hold. My audience is design-savvy millennials.
ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.successMessage: You kept yourself in the driver's seat and used AI as a brainstorming partner, not a replacement. That's the creative sweet spot.

ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.instruction: Rewrite this lazy prompt to preserve creative ownership: 'Make me a logo'
ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.placeholder: Rewrite with creative direction and constraints...
ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.hint: I'm designing a logo for a sustainable surf brand called 'Salt & Soul.' Guide me through 3 concept directions — one minimal, one hand-drawn, one geometric. For each, suggest a color palette and a typeface pairing. I'll choose the direction.
ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.goodExample: I run a vintage record shop called 'Groove Theory.' Help me explore 3 logo directions: retro 70s, modern minimalist, and hand-lettered. For each, describe the vibe, suggest 2 fonts, and a 3-color palette. I want to choose the direction myself.
ai-wont-replace-your-creativity.successMessage: See how you stayed the creative director? AI proposes, you decide. Your taste is the irreplaceable ingredient.

your-first-ai-creative-session.instruction: Write a prompt for your first AI creative session. Include your medium, a specific project, and ask AI to play a defined role (collaborator, critic, muse, researcher).
your-first-ai-creative-session.hint: Act as my songwriting collaborator. I'm writing a folk song about leaving a small town. I have the chorus melody but need help with verse lyrics. My style is conversational, like Phoebe Bridgers meets John Prine. Give me 3 verse options that use concrete imagery, not abstract emotions.
your-first-ai-creative-session.goodExample: Act as my creative researcher. I'm a muralist planning a piece about the history of jazz in New Orleans. Give me 5 key visual moments from 1920-1960 that would work as large-scale wall panels. Include the mood, key figures, and a color palette suggestion for each.
your-first-ai-creative-session.successMessage: Giving AI a clear role transforms it from a generic tool into a specialized creative partner. Great first session setup.

your-first-ai-creative-session.instruction: Write a prompt that uses AI to overcome a creative block. Describe what you're stuck on and ask for a specific type of help.
your-first-ai-creative-session.placeholder: I'm stuck on... Help me by...
your-first-ai-creative-session.hint: I'm stuck on the opening scene of my short film. The story is about a retired astronaut who runs a laundromat. I can't figure out how to show both worlds in the first 30 seconds without dialogue. Give me 5 visual-only opening shots that establish this duality.
your-first-ai-creative-session.successMessage: Creative blocks are where AI shines brightest — not replacing your vision, but offering angles you haven't considered.

ai-for-writers.instruction: Write a prompt asking AI to help you edit or improve a piece of writing. Include your voice/style preferences and what specifically needs work.
ai-for-writers.placeholder: Review my writing and focus on...
ai-for-writers.hint: Review this blog post draft about morning routines. Focus on: (1) cutting any sentence that doesn't earn its place, (2) making the opening hook sharper, (3) adding more sensory details to the 'coffee ritual' paragraph. Keep my casual, slightly sarcastic tone. Don't add corporate buzzwords.
ai-for-writers.goodExample: Edit this essay for a literary magazine. My voice is spare and Hemingway-influenced — short sentences, no adverbs. Flag any sentence over 15 words. Suggest cuts, not additions. Preserve the cold, observational tone. Don't soften the ending.
ai-for-writers.successMessage: Telling AI your style rules means it edits with your voice, not its default. That's how writers use AI without losing themselves.

ai-for-writers.instruction: Write a prompt using the 'AI-first' workflow: ask AI to generate a rough draft, then give instructions for how YOU will refine it.
ai-for-writers.hint: Draft a 300-word product description for handmade soy candles. Target: women aged 25-40 who value sustainability. Include sensory language about the scents (lavender, cedar, vanilla). I'll rewrite the voice to match my brand — just give me the structure and selling points to work from.
ai-for-writers.successMessage: The AI-first workflow: let AI build the scaffolding, then you bring the soul. Efficient and authentic.

ai-for-visual-artists.instruction: Write a prompt describing a visual concept to AI. Include artistic style, mood, composition details, and color direction.
ai-for-visual-artists.hint: Describe a book cover concept for a dystopian novel set in a flooded Tokyo. Style: muted Ghibli-inspired watercolor. Composition: low angle looking up at a torii gate half-submerged, with a small boat in the foreground. Palette: desaturated teals, rust, and cream. Mood: melancholy but hopeful.
ai-for-visual-artists.goodExample: Design a poster concept for a jazz festival. Style: mid-century modern, Saul Bass influence. Composition: a saxophone silhouette made of geometric shapes. Colors: midnight blue, burnt orange, cream. Include negative space. Typography: bold sans-serif, all caps. Mood: smoky, sophisticated, alive.
ai-for-visual-artists.successMessage: Visual prompting is its own art form. Specificity about style, mood, and composition is what separates generic output from work that matches your vision.

ai-for-visual-artists.instruction: Write a prompt asking AI to help you develop a visual series or collection with consistent style guidelines.
ai-for-visual-artists.placeholder: Help me develop a series of...
ai-for-visual-artists.hint: Help me develop a 5-piece illustration series about urban loneliness. Each piece features a single figure in a vast city space. Consistent rules: isometric perspective, limited palette (3 colors per piece from the same cool family), no faces shown, one warm accent object per scene that hints at connection.
ai-for-visual-artists.successMessage: Establishing visual rules for a series is advanced creative direction. AI can maintain consistency when you define the constraints clearly.

ai-for-musicians.instruction: Write a prompt asking AI to help with a specific music composition or production task. Include genre, mood, and technical details.
ai-for-musicians.hint: I'm producing a lo-fi hip-hop track at 85 BPM in D minor. Suggest a 4-chord progression for the verse that feels nostalgic but not sad. Include voicings — I want jazzy extensions (7ths, 9ths). Also suggest a contrasting 2-chord bridge that lifts the energy.
ai-for-musicians.goodExample: I'm writing a folk-rock song in G major, 120 BPM. The lyrics are about resilience after loss. Suggest a fingerpicking pattern for acoustic guitar that works with a G-Em-C-D progression. I want it to feel like early Bon Iver — intimate and raw.
ai-for-musicians.successMessage: Technical specificity in music prompts — key, tempo, genre references — gives AI enough to be genuinely useful as a composition partner.

ai-for-musicians.instruction: Write a prompt asking AI to help you write lyrics. Include the theme, your influences, and any structural requirements.
ai-for-musicians.placeholder: Write lyrics for a song about...
ai-for-musicians.hint: Write a second verse for my indie pop song about outgrowing your hometown. Verse 1 established the narrator driving past their old school. Verse 2 should move to the town diner. Match the syllable count of verse 1 (8-6-8-6 pattern). Rhyme scheme: ABAB. Tone: bittersweet, not bitter. Influences: Brandi Carlile, Hozier.
ai-for-musicians.successMessage: Great lyric prompting. Giving AI your structure, tone, and influences means the output actually fits your song instead of sounding generic.

ai-powered-content-creation-pipeline.instruction: Write a prompt that creates a complete content pipeline: ideation, drafting, and repurposing from one core idea.
ai-powered-content-creation-pipeline.placeholder: Take this one idea and create a pipeline...
ai-powered-content-creation-pipeline.hint: I run a pottery studio. Take the theme 'why imperfection is beautiful in handmade ceramics' and create: (1) 5 blog post angles, (2) a 200-word Instagram caption for the best angle, (3) 3 tweet-length quotes I can use across the week, (4) a YouTube video title and 5-point outline. Keep my brand voice: warm, artisan, anti-corporate.
ai-powered-content-creation-pipeline.goodExample: Take my core topic 'the mental health benefits of cooking from scratch' and build a content pipeline: (1) a 500-word newsletter draft, (2) repurpose into 3 Instagram carousel slides, (3) a 60-second TikTok script, (4) 2 Pinterest pin descriptions. Audience: millennial women. Tone: encouraging, evidence-backed, not preachy.
ai-powered-content-creation-pipeline.successMessage: One idea, multiple platforms — that's a real content pipeline. This approach saves hours of creative work every week.

ai-powered-content-creation-pipeline.instruction: Write a prompt that sets up a repeatable content workflow with templates and brand voice guidelines.
ai-powered-content-creation-pipeline.hint: Create a weekly content template for my tattoo studio's Instagram. Every week I need: Monday (artist spotlight), Wednesday (design process video script), Friday (client story post). For each, give me a fill-in-the-blank template with my brand voice rules: raw, authentic, zero corporate language, always end with a question.
ai-powered-content-creation-pipeline.successMessage: Templates turn a one-time prompt into an ongoing system. This is how creators scale without burning out.

building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.instruction: Write a prompt that builds a personalized creative toolkit — a set of reusable prompts tailored to your creative discipline.
building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.placeholder: Build me a toolkit of prompts for...
building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.hint: Build me a creative toolkit for freelance UX writing. I need 5 reusable prompt templates for: (1) writing microcopy for error states, (2) generating button label alternatives, (3) creating onboarding flows, (4) A/B testing headline options, (5) writing accessibility-friendly alt text. Each template should have [PLACEHOLDERS] for project-specific details.
building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.goodExample: Build me a prompt toolkit for documentary filmmaking. I need templates for: (1) interview question generation based on [SUBJECT BACKGROUND], (2) structuring a narrative arc from raw footage notes, (3) writing grant application summaries, (4) creating shot lists from script pages. Include [PLACEHOLDERS] so I can reuse them per project.
building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.successMessage: A personalized toolkit means you never start from scratch. These templates become your creative infrastructure.

building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.instruction: Describe your ideal creative AI workflow in 3-5 sentences. What would AI handle, what stays human, and where do they meet?
building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.placeholder: In my ideal creative workflow, AI would...
building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.hint: In my ideal workflow, AI handles research, reference gathering, and first-draft structure. I handle all final creative decisions, voice, and emotional tone. They meet at the editing stage — AI suggests cuts and improvements, I accept or reject each one. The rule: AI proposes, I dispose.
building-your-creative-ai-toolkit.successMessage: Knowing your boundaries with AI is a creative superpower. This clarity will serve you in every project.

the-art-of-creative-prompting.instruction: Write an advanced creative prompt that includes: a reference artist/style, emotional texture, constraints, and an iterative instruction.
the-art-of-creative-prompting.hint: Write a 100-word flash fiction piece about a lighthouse keeper's last night. Style: Raymond Carver minimalism — show, don't tell. Constraint: no adjectives. Emotional texture: quiet resignation. After the first draft, rewrite it once more but remove another 20% of the words without losing the meaning.
the-art-of-creative-prompting.goodExample: Write a monologue for a tired nurse ending a double shift. Style: the raw honesty of Ocean Vuong's prose. Constraint: under 150 words, no medical jargon. Emotional texture: exhaustion braided with purpose. After the first version, rewrite it as if she's speaking to her younger self instead of the audience.
the-art-of-creative-prompting.successMessage: Layered prompts with style references, constraints, and iteration loops — this is how you get AI output that actually feels authored.

the-art-of-creative-prompting.instruction: This prompt is too generic: 'Write a poem about nature.' Rewrite it with creative specificity that could produce something genuinely interesting.
the-art-of-creative-prompting.hint: Write a 12-line poem about the exact moment a wave pulls back from shore. Form: loose couplets. Voice: second person ('you'). Use only concrete nouns — no abstractions like 'beauty' or 'peace.' Include one line that's just a sound. Influence: Mary Oliver's attention to small things.
the-art-of-creative-prompting.goodExample: Write a haiku sequence (5 haiku) about a city garden in February. Each haiku must contain one man-made object and one living thing. Tone: the quiet surprise of finding life where you didn't expect it. Final haiku should echo the first but with a shift in perspective.
the-art-of-creative-prompting.successMessage: The distance between 'write a poem about nature' and your version is the distance between generic and genuinely creative. Specificity is your superpower.

ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.instruction: Write a prompt that uses AI ethically — asking it to help you develop YOUR original idea rather than copying existing work.
ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.placeholder: Help me develop my original concept...
ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.hint: I have an original concept: a graphic novel where each chapter is drawn in a different art style to represent the protagonist's shifting mental states. Help me map 6 mental states to 6 distinct visual styles. Don't reference specific copyrighted works — suggest art movements and techniques instead.
ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.badExample: Make something like that viral thing I saw
ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.goodExample: I have an original concept for a photo series: portraits of people holding the object they'd save in a fire. Help me develop a visual style guide — lighting, composition rules, and a consistent editing approach. Reference techniques from street photography, not specific photographers' copyrighted styles.
ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.successMessage: Using AI to develop YOUR ideas while respecting others' creative work — that's ethical creative AI use in action.

ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.instruction: Write a brief creative ethics statement: your personal rules for using AI in your creative work. Cover attribution, originality, and what stays human.
ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.placeholder: My creative AI ethics: I will always...
ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.hint: My creative AI ethics: I use AI for research, brainstorming, and first drafts only. All final creative decisions are mine. I never present AI-generated work as fully human-made without disclosure. I don't ask AI to replicate living artists' styles. My voice, taste, and lived experience are what make the work mine.
ethics-originality-and-your-creative-ide.successMessage: A personal ethics statement is your compass. It keeps you grounded as AI capabilities evolve.
